Name: radians
Description: Converts degrees to radians, i.e., (π / 180) ⋅ degrees.

Name: degrees
Description: Converts radians to degrees, i.e., (180 / π) ⋅ radians.

Name: sin
Description: The standard trigonometric sine function.

Name: cos
Description: The standard trigonometric cosine function.

Name: tan
Description: The standard trigonometric tangent.

Name: asin
Description: Arc sine. Returns an angle whose sine is x.

Name: acos
Description: Arc cosine. Returns an angle whose cosine is x.

Name: atan
Description: Arc tangent. Returns an angle whose tangent is y / x.

Name: sinh
Description: Returns the hyperbolic sine function (ex - e-x) / 2.

Name: cosh
Description: Returns the hyperbolic cosine function (ex + e-x) / 2.

Name: tanh
Description: Returns the hyperbolic tangent function sinh(x) / cosh(x).

Name: asinh
Description: Arc hyperbolic sine; returns the inverse of sinh.

Name: acosh
Description: Arc hyperbolic cosine. Returns the non-negative inverse of cosh.

Name: atanh
Description: Arc hyperbolic tangent. Returns the inverse of tanh.

Name: pow
Description: Returns x raised to the y power, i.e., xy.

Name: exp
Description: Returns the natural exponentiation of x, i.e., ex.

Name: log
Description:
Returns the natural logarithm of x, i.e., returns the value y
which satisfies the equation x = ey.

Name: exp2
Description: Returns 2 raised to the x power, i.e., 2x.

Name: log2
Description:
Returns the base 2 logarithm of x, i.e., returns the value y which
satisfies the equation x = 2y.

Name: sqrt
Description: Returns sqrt(x).

Name: sqrt
Description: Returns sqrt(x).

Name: abs
Description: Returns x if x ≥ 0; otherwise it returns -x.

Name: abs
Description: Returns x if x ≥ 0; otherwise it returns -x.

Name: abs
Description: Returns x if x ≥ 0; otherwise it returns -x.

Name: sign
Description: Returns 1.0 if x > 0, 0.0 if x = 0, or -1.0 if x < 0.

Name: sign
Description: Returns 1.0 if x > 0, 0.0 if x = 0, or -1.0 if x < 0.

Name: sign
Description: Returns 1.0 if x > 0, 0.0 if x = 0, or -1.0 if x < 0.

Name: floor
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer that is less than or equal to x.

Name: floor
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer that is less than or equal to x.

Name: trunc
Description:
Returns a value equal to the nearest integer to x whose absolute
value is not larger than the absolute value of x.

Name: trunc
Description:
Returns a value equal to the nearest integer to x whose absolute
value is not larger than the absolute value of x.

Name: round
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer to x.

Name: round
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer to x.

Name: ceil
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer that is greater than or equal to x.

Name: ceil
Description: Returns a value equal to the nearest integer that is greater than or equal to x.

Name: fract
Description: Returns x - floor(x).

Name: fract
Description: Returns x - floor(x).

Name: mod
Description: Modulus. Returns x - y ⋅ floor(x / y).

Name: mod
Description: Modulus. Returns x - y ⋅ floor(x / y).

Name: mod
Description: Modulus. Returns x - y ⋅ floor(x / y).

Name: mod

Name: modf
Description:
Returns the fractional part of x and sets i to the integer part (as
a whole number floating-point value).

Name: modf
Description:
Returns the fractional part of x and sets i to the integer part (as
a whole number floating-point value).

Name: mix
Description: Returns the linear blend of x and y, i.e., x ⋅ (1 - a) + y ⋅ a.

Name: mix
Description: Returns the linear blend of x and y, i.e., x ⋅ (1 - a) + y ⋅ a.

Name: mix
Description: Returns the linear blend of x and y, i.e., x ⋅ (1 - a) + y ⋅ a.

Name: mix
Description: Returns the linear blend of x and y, i.e., x ⋅ (1 - a) + y ⋅ a.

Name: step
Description: Returns 0.0 if x < edge; otherwise it returns 1.0.

Name: step
Description: Returns 0.0 if x < edge; otherwise it returns 1.0.

Name: smoothstep
Description:
Returns 0.0 if x ≤ edge0 and 1.0 if x ≥ edge1, and
performs smooth Hermite interpolation between 0 and 1 when edge0 < x < edge1.

Returns 0.0 if x ≤ edge0 and 1.0 if x ≥ edge1, and

Description: Computes and returns a * b + c.

Name: length
Description: Returns the length of vector x, i.e., sqrt( x02 + x12 + …​ ).

Name: length
Description: Returns the length of vector x, i.e., sqrt( x02 + x12 + …​ ).

Name: distance
Description: Returns the distance between p0 and p1, i.e., length(p0 - p1)

Name: distance
Description: Returns the distance between p0 and p1, i.e., length(p0 - p1)

Name: dot
Description: Returns the dot product of x and y, i.e., x0 ⋅ y0 + x1 ⋅ y1 + …​

Name: dot
Description: Returns the dot product of x and y, i.e., x0 ⋅ y0 + x1 ⋅ y1 + …​

Name: cross
Description:
Returns the cross product of x and y, i.e.,
      (x1 ⋅ y2 - y1 ⋅ x2,
            x2 ⋅ y0 - y2 ⋅ x0,
            x0 ⋅ y1 - y0 ⋅ x1).

Name: cross
Description:
Returns the cross product of x and y, i.e.,
      (x1 ⋅ y2 - y1 ⋅ x2,
            x2 ⋅ y0 - y2 ⋅ x0,
            x0 ⋅ y1 - y0 ⋅ x1).

Name: normalize
Description: Returns a vector in the same direction as x but with a length of 1, i.e. x / length(x).

Name: normalize
Description: Returns a vector in the same direction as x but with a length of 1, i.e. x / length(x).

Name: ftransform
Description: Available only when using the compatibility profile. For core OpenGL, use invariant.

Name: reflect
Description:
For the incident vector I and surface orientation N, returns the reflection direction:
I - 2 ⋅ dot(N, I) ⋅ N. N must already be normalized in order to achieve
the desired result.

Name: reflect
Description:
For the incident vector I and surface orientation N, returns the reflection direction:
I - 2 ⋅ dot(N, I) ⋅ N. N must already be normalized in order to achieve
the desired result.

Name: refract
Description:
For the incident vector I and surface normal N, and the ratio of indices of refraction
eta, return the refraction vector. The result is computed by the refraction equation shown below.

Name: refract
Description:
For the incident vector I and surface normal N, and the ratio of indices of refraction
eta, return the refraction vector. The result is computed by the refraction equation shown below.

Name: matrixCompMult
Description:
Multiply matrix x by matrix y component-wise, i.e., result[i][j]
is the scalar product of x[i][j] and y[i][j].

Name: outerProduct
Description:
Treats the first parameter c as a column vector (matrix with one
column) and the second parameter r as a row vector (matrix with one
row) and does a linear algebraic matrix multiply c * r, yielding a
matrix whose number of rows is the number of components in c and
whose number of columns is the number of components in r.

row) and does a linear algebraic matrix multiply c * r, yielding a
matrix whose number of rows is the number of components in c and
whose number of columns is the number of components in r.

Name: uaddCarry
Description: Adds 32-bit unsigned integers x and y, returning the sum modulo 232.

Name: usubBorrow
Description:
Subtracts the 32-bit unsigned integer y from x, returning the difference if non-negative, or
232 plus the difference otherwise.

Name: bitfieldExtract
Description:
Extracts bits [offset, offset + bits - 1] from value, returning them in the least
significant bits of the result.

Name: bitfieldExtract
Description:
Extracts bits [offset, offset + bits - 1] from value, returning them in the least
significant bits of the result.

Name: bitfieldInsert
Description: Inserts the bits least significant bits of insert into base.

Name: bitfieldInsert
Description: Inserts the bits least significant bits of insert into base.

Name: bitfieldReverse
Description: Reverses the bits of value.

Name: bitfieldReverse
Description: Reverses the bits of value.

Name: bitCount
Description: Returns the number of one bits in the binary representation of value.

Name: bitCount
Description: Returns the number of one bits in the binary representation of value.

Name: findLSB
Description: Returns the bit number of the least significant one bit in the binary representation of value.

Name: findMSB
Description: Returns the bit number of the most significant bit in the binary representation of value.

Name: findMSB
Description: Returns the bit number of the most significant bit in the binary representation of value.

Description: Computes a new value by adding the value of data to the contents mem.

Name: atomicOr
Description: Computes a new value by performing a bit-wise OR of the value of data and the contents of mem.

Description: Computes a new value by performing a bit-wise OR of the value of data and the contents of mem.

Name: atomicXor
Description:
Computes a new value by performing a bit-wise EXCLUSIVE OR of the value of data and the contents
of mem.

Computes a new value by performing a bit-wise EXCLUSIVE OR of the value of data and the contents

Name: atomicExchange
Description: Computes a new value by simply copying the value of data.

Name: atomicExchange

Name: atomicCompSwap
Description:
Compares the value of compare and the contents of mem. If the values are equal, the new value
is given by data; otherwise, it is taken from the original contents of mem.

imageSamples
Description: Returns the number of samples of the image bound to image.

Name: imageSamples
Description: Returns the number of samples of the image bound to image.

Name: imageLoad
Description: Loads the texel at the coordinate P from the image unit image (in IMAGE_PARAMS).

Name: imageStore
Description:
Stores data into the texel at the coordinate P from the image specified by image.
For multisample stores, the sample number is given by sample. When image, P, and sample
identify a valid texel, the bits used to represent data are converted to the format of the image
unit in the manner described in section
8.26
“Texture Image Loads and Stores” of the OpenGL Specification and stored to the specified
texel.

Name: imageAtomicAdd
Description: Computes a new value by adding the value of data to the contents of the selected texel.

Name: imageAtomicAdd
Description: Computes a new value by adding the value of data to the contents of the selected texel.

Name: imageAtomicMin
Description:
Computes a new value by taking the minimum of the value of data and the contents of the selected
texel.

texel.

Name: imageAtomicMax
Description:
Computes a new value by taking the maximum of the value data and the contents of the selected
texel.

Description:
Compares the value of compare and the contents of the selected texel. If the values are equal,
the new value is given by data; otherwise, it is taken from the original value loaded from the
texel.

Name: EmitStreamVertex
Description:
Emits the current values of output variables to the current output primitive on stream stream.
The argument to stream must be a constant integral expression. On return from this call, the
values of all output variables are undefined.

Name: EndStreamPrimitive
Description:
Completes the current output primitive on stream stream and starts a new one. The argument to
stream must be a constant integral expression. No vertex is emitted.

Name: EmitVertex
Description:
Emits the current values of output variables to the current output primitive.
When multiple output streams are supported, this is equivalent to calling EmitStreamVertex(0).

Name: EndPrimitive
Description:
Completes the current output primitive and starts a new one.
When multiple output streams are supported, this is equivalent to calling EndStreamPrimitive(0).

Name: dFdx
Description:
Returns either dFdxFine(p) or dFdxCoarse(p), based on implementation choice, presumably
whichever is the faster, or by whichever is selected in the API through quality-versus-speed hints.

Name: dFdy
Description:
Returns either dFdyFine(p) or dFdyCoarse(p), based on implementation choice, presumably
whichever is the faster, or by whichever is selected in the API through quality-versus-speed hints.

Name: dFdxFine
Description:
Returns the partial derivative of p with respect to the window x coordinate. Will use local
differencing based on the value of p for the current fragment and its immediate neighbor(s).

Name: dFdyFine
Description:
Returns the partial derivative of p with respect to the window y coordinate. Will use local
differencing based on the value of p for the current fragment and its immediate neighbor(s).

Name: dFdxCoarse
Description:
Returns the partial derivative of p with respect to the window x coordinate. Will use local
differencing based on the value of p for the current fragment’s neighbors, and will possibly, but
not necessarily, include the value of p for the current fragment.

Name: dFdyCoarse
Description:
Returns the partial derivative of p with respect to the window y coordinate. Will use local
differencing based on the value of p for the current fragment’s neighbors, and will possibly, but
not necessarily, include the value of p for the current fragment.

Name: fwidth
Description: Returns abs(dFdx(p)) + abs(dFdy(p)).

Name: fwidthFine
Description: Returns abs(dFdxFine(p)) + abs(dFdyFine(p)).

Name: fwidthCoarse
Description: Returns abs(dFdxCoarse(p)) + abs(dFdyCoarse(p)).

Name: interpolateAtCentroid
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant sampled at a location inside both the pixel and the
primitive being processed. The value obtained would be the same value assigned to the input
variable if declared with the centroid qualifier.

Name: interpolateAtCentroid
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant sampled at a location inside both the pixel and the
primitive being processed. The value obtained would be the same value assigned to the input
variable if declared with the centroid qualifier.

Name: interpolateAtCentroid
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant sampled at a location inside both the pixel and the
primitive being processed. The value obtained would be the same value assigned to the input
variable if declared with the centroid qualifier.

Name: interpolateAtCentroid
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant sampled at a location inside both the pixel and the
primitive being processed. The value obtained would be the same value assigned to the input
variable if declared with the centroid qualifier.

Name: interpolateAtSample
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable at the location of sample number sample.
If multisample buffers are not available, the input variable will be evaluated at the center of the
pixel. If sample sample does not exist, the position used to interpolate the input variable is undefined.

Name: interpolateAtSample
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable at the location of sample number sample.
If multisample buffers are not available, the input variable will be evaluated at the center of the
pixel. If sample sample does not exist, the position used to interpolate the input variable is undefined.

Name: interpolateAtSample
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable at the location of sample number sample.
If multisample buffers are not available, the input variable will be evaluated at the center of the
pixel. If sample sample does not exist, the position used to interpolate the input variable is undefined.

Name: interpolateAtSample
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable at the location of sample number sample.
If multisample buffers are not available, the input variable will be evaluated at the center of the
pixel. If sample sample does not exist, the position used to interpolate the input variable is undefined.

Name: interpolateAtOffset
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable sampled at an offset from the center of the
pixel specified by offset. The two floating-point components of offset, give the offset in
pixels in the x and y directions, respectively.

Name: interpolateAtOffset
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable sampled at an offset from the center of the
pixel specified by offset. The two floating-point components of offset, give the offset in
pixels in the x and y directions, respectively.

Name: interpolateAtOffset
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable sampled at an offset from the center of the
pixel specified by offset. The two floating-point components of offset, give the offset in
pixels in the x and y directions, respectively.

Name: interpolateAtOffset
Description:
Returns the value of the input interpolant variable sampled at an offset from the center of the
pixel specified by offset. The two floating-point components of offset, give the offset in
pixels in the x and y directions, respectively.

Name: noise1
Description: Returns a 1D noise value based on the input value x.

Name: noise2
Description: Returns a 2D noise value based on the input value x.

Name: noise3
Description: Returns a 3D noise value based on the input value x.

Name: noise4
Description: Returns a 4D noise value based on the input value x.

Name: barrier
Description:
For any given static instance of barrier(), all tessellation control shader invocations for a
single input patch must enter it before any will be allowed to continue beyond it, or all compute
shader invocations for a single workgroup must enter it before any will continue beyond it.

Name: memoryBarrier
Description: Control the ordering of memory transactions issued by a single shader invocation.

Name: memoryBarrierAtomicCounter
Description: Control the ordering of accesses to atomic-counter variables issued by a single shader invocation.

Name: memoryBarrierBuffer
Description: Control the ordering of memory transactions to buffer variables issued within a single shader invocation.

Name: memoryBarrierShared
Description:
Control the ordering of memory transactions to shared variables issued within a single shader
invocation, as viewed by other invocations in the same workgroup.

Name: memoryBarrierImage
Description: Control the ordering of memory transactions to images issued within a single shader invocation.

Name: groupMemoryBarrier
Description:
Control the ordering of all memory transactions issued within a single shader invocation, as viewed
by other invocations in the same workgroup.

Name: subpassLoad
Description:
Read from a subpass input, from the implicit location (x, y, layer) of the current fragment
coordinate.

Name: subpassLoad
Description:
Read from a subpass input, from the implicit location (x, y, layer) of the current fragment
coordinate.

Name: anyInvocation
Description: Returns true if and only if value is true for at least one active invocation in the group.

Name: allInvocations
Description: Returns true if and only if value is true for all active invocations in the group.

Name: allInvocationsEqual
Description: Returns true if value is the same for all active invocations in the group.
